description_long: This object is a small bird with a compact body, predominantly yellow on its underparts and flanks, and olive-yellow to greenish tones on its back and wings. The head is a pale gray, contrasting with the brighter yellow body, and the beak is short and conical, suited for seed eating. The wings are folded tightly against the body, and the tail is straight and moderately long, tapering to a subtle point. The legs and feet are slender and dark, tucked beneath the body. The plumage appears smooth and well-groomed, with no distinct barring or spotting, giving the bird a clean, uniform appearance. This bird would be commonly seen perched or foraging in urban parks, gardens, or along city streets.
description: A small, yellow-bodied bird with a pale gray head, conical beak, and smooth, uniform plumage. Its wings and tail are streamlined, and the legs are dark and slender.
description_view_0: Side view showing the bird's yellow body, gray head, and streamlined tail, with wings folded close to the body.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the rounded head, conical beak, and symmetrical body shape.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same yellow and gray coloration, with the tail and legs more visible.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the tail's shape and the alignment of the wings along the back.
attribute_plumage_pattern: solid yellow underparts with olive-yellow back and pale gray head, no distinct bars or spots
